framework.name: Cryptographic Agent Message Signing Protocol
framework.description: A protocol for multi-agent communication using SHA-256 hashing and digital signatures to ensure message authenticity and prevent tampering. Agents use cryptographic key pairs (generated from seed passwords) to sign message digests, which recipients verify before processing. Implemented through identity verification, message encoding, and signature validation.
framework.raw_quote: the purpose of this protocol is for our microagents to exchange messages with one another and sign their messages to ensure authenticity and prevent tampering
